revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kayanarumi_no_Kami]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''カヤナルミ'''とは、[[延喜式]]『出雲国造神賀詞』では賀夜奈流美、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では加夜奈留美とされている女神。
[[古事記]]、[[日本書紀]]にはその名はない。『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とある。『延喜式交替式』、『類聚三代格』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考１] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:日本の神|かやなるみ]]

revision.comment: 鳥鳴海神などのの関連
revision.text:
'''カヤナルミ'''とは、[[延喜式]]『出雲国造神賀詞』では賀夜奈流美、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では加夜奈留美とされている女神。
『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とある。『延喜式交替式』、『類聚三代格』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。[[古事記]]、[[日本書紀]]にはその名はないが、古事記の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する鳥鳴海神（トリナルミ）や布忍富鳥鳴海神（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考１] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:日本の神|かやなるみ]]

'''カヤナルミ'''とは、『[[延喜式]]』「[[祝詞]]式」にある『[[出雲国造神賀詞]]』では賀夜奈流美、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では加夜奈留美とされている女神。ただし男神とする説もある。
『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とあり、[[事代主]]など３柱の神とともに[[天皇家]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』、『類聚三代格』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。[[古事記]]、[[日本書紀]]にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、古事記の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する鳥鳴海神（トリナルミ）や布忍富鳥鳴海神（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考１] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:日本の神|かやなるみ]]

'''カヤナルミ'''とは、『[[延喜式]]』「[[祝詞]]式」にある『[[出雲国造神賀詞]]』では賀夜奈流美、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では加夜奈留美とされている女神。ただし男神とする説もある。
『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とあり、[[大物主]]くしみかたま命、その子あぢすき高ひこねの命（[[アヂスキタカヒコネ]]）、[[事代主]]とともに[[天皇家]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』、『類聚三代格』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。[[古事記]]、[[日本書紀]]にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、古事記の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する鳥鳴海神（トリナルミ）や布忍富鳥鳴海神（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考１] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:日本の神|かやなるみ]]

『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とあり、「[[大物主]]櫛長瓦玉命」、「阿遅須伎高孫根<small>乃<big>命」（[[アヂスキタカヒコネ]]）、[[事代主]]とともに[[天皇家]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』、『類聚三代格』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。[[古事記]]、[[日本書紀]]にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、古事記の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する鳥鳴海神（トリナルミ）や布忍富鳥鳴海神（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。

『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。

『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
== 神社 == *飛鳥坐神社 - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *加夜奈留美命神社 - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 玉櫛姫 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲神[[神武天皇|日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考１] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:日本の神|かやなるみ]]

『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。なお名義は未詳であるが、奈流美と同音の鳴海は鳴り響く海の意とする説がある<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
==系譜== :[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]である[[須佐氏]]の系譜では、[[大国主神]]の御子神の一柱に賀夜奈流美命の名が見え、子に[[国忍富神|国忍富命]]、孫に[[雲山命]]と続き、さらにその子孫が須佐氏になる。 :母神は不明。
== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 [[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]] 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」、『[[旧事本紀]]』では「都味歯八重事代主神 化爲八尋熊鰐通三嶋溝杭女活玉依姫 生一男一女（略）」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1]

revision.text:
'''カヤナルミ'''（賀夜奈流美命）とは、『[[延喜式]]』「[[祝詞]]式」にある『[[出雲国造神賀詞]]』に登場する[[神]]。 神賀詞では賀夜奈流美、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では加夜奈留美とされている女[[神]]。ただし男神とする説もある。
==概要== 『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とあり、「[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]」、「[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根<small>乃</small>命]]」、[[事代主]]とともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』、『[[類聚三代格]]』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。
『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。なお名義は未詳であるが、奈流美と同音の鳴海は鳴り響く海の意とする説がある<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
==系譜== [[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]である[[須佐氏]]の系譜では、[[大国主神]]の御子神の一柱に賀夜奈流美命の名が見え、子に[[国忍富神|国忍富命]]、孫に[[雲山命]]と続き、さらにその子孫が須佐氏になる。
母神は不明。
== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 [[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]] 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」、『[[旧事本紀]]』では「都味歯八重事代主神 化爲八尋熊鰐通三嶋溝杭女活玉依姫 生一男一女（略）」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:女神]]

==概要== 『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とあり、「[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]」、「[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根<small>乃</small>命]]」、[[事代主]]とともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』、『[[類聚三代格]]』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。
『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。なお名義は未詳であるが、奈流美と同音の鳴海は鳴り響く海の意とする説がある<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
==系譜== [[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]である[[須佐氏]]の系譜では、[[大国主神]]の御子神の一柱に賀夜奈流美命の名が見え、子に[[国忍富神|国忍富命]]、孫に[[雲山命]]と続き、さらにその子孫が須佐氏になる。

== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 [[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]] 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」、『[[旧事本紀]]』では「都味歯八重事代主神 化爲八尋熊鰐通三嶋溝杭女活玉依姫 生一男一女（略）」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。

==系譜== [[大物主命]]の子とされ、[[賀屋鳴比女命]]との間に[[鴨主命]]を生んだとされる<ref name="keifu"/>。
また、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]である[[須佐氏]]の系譜では、[[大国主神]]の御子神の一柱に賀夜奈流美命の名が見え、子に[[国忍富神|国忍富命]]、孫に[[雲山命]]と続き、さらにその子孫が須佐氏になる。
== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 [[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]] 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」、『[[旧事本紀]]』では「都味歯八重事代主神 化爲八尋熊鰐通三嶋溝杭女活玉依姫 生一男一女（略）」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。

== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 別名 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = [[飛鳥坐神社]] など | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = [[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''カヤナルミ'''（賀夜奈流美命）とは、『[[延喜式]]』「[[祝詞]]式」にある『[[出雲国造神賀詞]]』に登場する[[神]]。『神賀詞』では'''賀夜奈流美命'''と表記され、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では'''加夜奈留美命'''と表記されている。飛鳥坐神社・加夜奈留美命神社では[[女神]]としている。
==概要== 『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」とあり、「[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]」、「[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根<small>乃</small>命]]」、[[事代主]]とともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』、『[[類聚三代格]]』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。
『[[古事記]]』、『[[日本書紀]]』にその名がないため、その出自は明らかではないが、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連が考えられる。なお名義は未詳であるが、奈流美と同音の鳴海は鳴り響く海の意とする説がある<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとある。
== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 [[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]] 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」、『[[旧事本紀]]』では「都味歯八重事代主神 化爲八尋熊鰐通三嶋溝杭女活玉依姫 生一男一女（略）」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1]

{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:大神氏]] [[Category:女神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 別名 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = [[飛鳥坐神社]] など | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = [[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''賀夜奈流美/加夜奈留美/賀屋鳴比女'''（カヤナルミ）とは、『[[延喜式]]』「[[祝詞]]式」にある『[[出雲国造神賀詞]]』に登場する[[神]]。『神賀詞』では'''賀夜奈流美命'''と表記され、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では'''加夜奈留美命'''と表記されている。飛鳥坐神社・加夜奈留美命神社では[[女神]]としている。
==概要== 『[[古事記]]』『[[日本書紀]]』に登場せず、その出自、名義、伝説についてはいずれも不明。
名義については、名前の「ナルミ」を同音の「鳴海（鳴り響く海）」の意とする説があり、また『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連も考えられているが、いずれも未詳である<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」と記述されており、「[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]」「[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根<small>乃</small>命]]」「[[事代主|事代主」]]とともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』『[[類聚三代格]]』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。
== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美命、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐 *:由緒は不明。ただし『[[日本書紀]]』第6の一書には「事代主神化爲八尋熊鰐 通三嶋溝樴姫 或云 [[玉櫛媛|玉櫛姫]] 而生兒[[ヒメタタライスズヒメ|姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命]] 是爲[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦火火出見天皇]]之后也」、『[[旧事本紀]]』では「都味歯八重事代主神 化爲八尋熊鰐通三嶋溝杭女活玉依姫 生一男一女（略）」とあり事代主神が八尋鰐と化したことの記述がある。

名義については、名前の「ナルミ」を同音の「鳴海（鳴り響く海）」の意とする説があり、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連も考えられているが未詳である<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
『出雲国造神賀詞』には「'''賀夜奈流美'''命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」と記述されており、「[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]」「[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根<small>乃</small>命]]」「[[事代主|事代主」]]とともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』『[[類聚三代格]]』には「賀屋鳴比女」とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元年([[859年]])[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。
== 祀る神社 == *[[飛鳥坐神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美命 *[[飛鳥神社]] - 祭神 事代主神、加夜奈留美、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真 *[[加夜奈留美命神社]] - 祭神 加夜奈留美命 *大行事社（[[大神神社]]の末社） - 祭神 加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐（由緒は不明）

『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元（[[859年|859]]）年[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。 == 祀る神社 == * 飛鳥坐神社（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]飛鳥） - 主祭神 : 加夜奈留美命を祀る。 * 加夜奈留美命神社（奈良県高市郡明日香村栢森） - 主祭神 : 加夜奈留美命を祀る。 * [[飛鳥神社]]（奈良県[[奈良市]]北京終町） - 主祭神 : 事代主神、加夜奈留美、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真を祀る。 * 大行事社（奈良県[[桜井市]]三輪平等寺） - 祭神 : [[大神神社]]の末社。加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐を祀る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html 飛鳥坐神社とは｜飛鳥坐神社公式ホームページ] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 加夜奈留美命神社(かやなるみのみことじんじや)とは？ 意味や使い方 - コトバンク] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html 大和国一宮・三輪明神 大神神社 奈良県] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:大神氏]] [[Category:女神]]

「出雲国造神賀詞」には「'''賀夜奈流美命'''乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」と記述されており、[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根乃命]]、[[事代主]]ともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』『[[類聚三代格]]』には賀屋鳴比女とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元（[[859年|859]]）年[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。 == 祀る神社 == * 飛鳥坐神社（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]飛鳥） - 主祭神 ** 加夜奈留美命を祀る。 * 加夜奈留美命神社（奈良県高市郡明日香村栢森） - 主祭神 ** 加夜奈留美命を祀る。 * [[飛鳥神社]]（奈良県[[奈良市]]北京終町） - 主祭神 ** 事代主神、加夜奈留美、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真を祀る。 * 大行事社（奈良県[[桜井市]]三輪平等寺） - 祭神 ** [[大神神社]]の末社。加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐を祀る。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html 飛鳥坐神社とは｜飛鳥坐神社公式ホームページ] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 加夜奈留美命神社(かやなるみのみことじんじや)とは？ 意味や使い方 - コトバンク] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html 大和国一宮・三輪明神 大神神社 奈良県] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:大神氏]] [[Category:女神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 賀夜奈流美命（カヤナルミノミコト） | 別名 =賀夜奈流美命、加夜奈留美命、賀屋鳴比女 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[飛鳥坐神社]] * 加夜奈留美命神社 * [[飛鳥神社]] * [[大神神社#末社|大行事社]] }} | 記紀等 = 延喜式 | 関連氏族 = [[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''カヤナルミ'''（賀夜奈流美、加夜奈留美、賀屋鳴比女）とは、『[[延喜式]]』に収録されている[[祝詞]]である「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」に登場する[[神]]。「出雲国造神賀詞」では'''賀夜奈流美命'''と表記され、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では'''加夜奈留美命'''と表記されている。飛鳥坐神社・加夜奈留美命神社では[[女神]]としている。 ==概要== 『[[古事記]]』『[[日本書紀]]』に登場せず、その出自、名義、伝説についてはいずれも不明。
名義については、名前の「ナルミ」を同音の「鳴海（鳴り響く海）」の意とする説があり、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[ヌノシトミトリナルミ|布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連も考えられているが未詳である<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
「出雲国造神賀詞」には「'''賀夜奈流美命'''乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」と記述されており、[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根乃命]]、[[事代主]]ともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』『[[類聚三代格]]』には賀屋鳴比女とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元（[[859年|859]]）年[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。 == 祀る神社 == * 飛鳥坐神社（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]飛鳥） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|加夜奈留美命を祀る。}} * 加夜奈留美命神社（奈良県高市郡明日香村栢森） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|加夜奈留美命を祀る。}} * [[飛鳥神社]]（奈良県[[奈良市]]北京終町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|事代主神、加夜奈留美、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真を祀る。}} * 大行事社（奈良県[[桜井市]]三輪平等寺） - 祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[大神神社]]の末社。加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐を祀る。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html 飛鳥坐神社とは｜飛鳥坐神社公式ホームページ] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 加夜奈留美命神社(かやなるみのみことじんじや)とは？ 意味や使い方 - コトバンク] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html 大和国一宮・三輪明神 大神神社 奈良県] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:大神氏]] [[Category:女神]]

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * [[Asuka Niimasu Shrine]] (飛鳥坐神社) * Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine (加夜奈留美命神社) * [[Asuka Shrine (Nara)]] (飛鳥神社) * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at [[Asuka Niimasu Shrine]] and Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
### Etymology One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
### Appearance in Texts In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* **[[Asuka Niimasu Shrine]] (飛鳥坐神社)**: Located in [[Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * **Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine**: Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * **[[Asuka Shrine]]**: Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * **Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)**: A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
### Etymology One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
### Appearance in Texts In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* **{{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}**: Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * **{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}**: Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}**: Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * **Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)**: A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka Niimasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

### Appearance in Texts In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* **{{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}**: Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * **{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}**: Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * **{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}**: Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * **Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)**: A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. == Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.

In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
### Etymology One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
### Appearance in Texts In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka Niimasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka Niimasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
### Etymology One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
### Appearance in Texts In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
### Etymology One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
### Appearance in Texts In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

The text states:

In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).

* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).

Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

| Rank =
| Attributes =
| Burial place =

* {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}
* {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社)
}}
| Texts = [[Engishiki]]
| Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]]
}}
'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes ==

== External Links ==

=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.

The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes ==

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (出雲国造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes ==

* {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}
* {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

* {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}
* {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社)
}}
| Texts = [[Engishiki]]
| Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]]
}}
'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies]
* [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine]
* [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank]
* [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

{{Infobox Japanese deity
| Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto)
| Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]]
| Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto)
| Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女
| Rank =
| Attributes =
| Burial place =
| Father =
| Mother =
| Relatives =

| Siblings =
| Children =
| Shrine = {{Plainlist|
* {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}
* {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}
* {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社)
}}
| Texts = [[Engishiki]]
| Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]]
}}
'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies]
* [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine]
* [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank]
* [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

{{Infobox Japanese deity
| Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto)
| Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]]

'''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天"
(*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines ==
Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity.
* '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village.
* '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
* '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

* [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine]
* [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank]
* [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

revision.comment: 48 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:カヤナルミ up to rev 101607236
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 賀夜奈流美命（カヤナルミノミコト） | 別名 =賀夜奈流美命、加夜奈留美命、賀屋鳴比女 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[飛鳥坐神社]] * 加夜奈留美命神社 * [[飛鳥神社]] * [[大神神社#末社|大行事社]] }} | 記紀等 = 延喜式 | 関連氏族 = [[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''カヤナルミ'''（賀夜奈流美、加夜奈留美、賀屋鳴比女）とは、『[[延喜式]]』に収録されている[[祝詞]]である「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」に登場する[[神]]。「出雲国造神賀詞」では'''賀夜奈流美命'''と表記され、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では'''加夜奈留美命'''と表記されている。飛鳥坐神社・加夜奈留美命神社では[[女神]]としている。 ==概要== 『[[古事記]]』『[[日本書紀]]』に登場せず、その出自、名義、伝説についてはいずれも不明。
名義については、名前の「ナルミ」を同音の「鳴海（鳴り響く海）」の意とする説があり、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[ヌノシトミトリナルミ|布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連も考えられているが未詳である<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
「出雲国造神賀詞」には「'''賀夜奈流美命'''乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」と記述されており、[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根乃命]]、[[事代主]]ともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』『[[類聚三代格]]』には賀屋鳴比女とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元（[[859年|859]]）年[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。 == 祀る神社 == * 飛鳥坐神社（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]飛鳥） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|加夜奈留美命を祀る。}} * 加夜奈留美命神社（奈良県高市郡明日香村栢森） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|加夜奈留美命を祀る。}} * [[飛鳥神社]]（奈良県[[奈良市]]北京終町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|事代主神、加夜奈留美、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真を祀る。}} * 大行事社（奈良県[[桜井市]]三輪平等寺） - 祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[大神神社]]の末社。加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐を祀る。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html 飛鳥坐神社とは｜飛鳥坐神社公式ホームページ] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 加夜奈留美命神社(かやなるみのみことじんじや)とは？ 意味や使い方 - コトバンク] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html 大和国一宮・三輪明神 大神神社 奈良県] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:大神氏]] [[Category:女神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 賀夜奈流美命 | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 賀夜奈流美命（カヤナルミノミコト） | 別名 =賀夜奈流美命、加夜奈留美命、賀屋鳴比女 | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[飛鳥坐神社]] * 加夜奈留美命神社 * [[飛鳥神社]] * [[大神神社#末社|大行事社]] }} | 記紀等 = 延喜式 | 関連氏族 = [[大神氏|三輪君]] }} '''カヤナルミ'''（賀夜奈流美、加夜奈留美、賀屋鳴比女）とは、『[[延喜式]]』に収録されている[[祝詞]]である「[[出雲国造神賀詞]]」に登場する[[神]]。「出雲国造神賀詞」では'''賀夜奈流美命'''と表記され、[[飛鳥坐神社]]・加夜奈留美命神社では'''加夜奈留美命'''と表記されている。飛鳥坐神社・加夜奈留美命神社では[[女神]]としている。 ==概要== 『[[古事記]]』『[[日本書紀]]』に登場せず、その出自、名義、伝説についてはいずれも不明。
名義については、名前の「ナルミ」を同音の「鳴海（鳴り響く海）」の意とする説があり、『古事記』の[[大国主]]の系図に登場する[[鳥鳴海神]]（トリナルミ）や[[ヌノシトミトリナルミ|布忍富鳥鳴海神]]（ヌノシトミトリナルミ）（どちらも男神）との関連も考えられているが未詳である<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記 神名釈義</ref>。
「出雲国造神賀詞」には「'''賀夜奈流美命'''乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天」と記述されており、[[大物主|大物主櫛瓶玉命]]、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|阿遅須伎高孫根乃命]]、[[事代主]]ともに[[皇室]]を守護する神として述べられている。『延喜式交替式』『[[類聚三代格]]』には賀屋鳴比女とある。
『[[日本三代実録]]』によると[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]元（[[859年|859]]）年[[1月27日 (旧暦)|正月二七日]]に[[大和国]]の賀夜奈流美神を従五位下から正四位下にしたとされる。 == 祀る神社 == * 飛鳥坐神社（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]飛鳥） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|加夜奈留美命を祀る。}} * 加夜奈留美命神社（奈良県高市郡明日香村栢森） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|加夜奈留美命を祀る。}} * [[飛鳥神社]]（奈良県[[奈良市]]北京終町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|事代主神、加夜奈留美、宇須多岐比女命、不足留比女命、菅原道真を祀る。}} * 大行事社（奈良県[[桜井市]]三輪平等寺） - 祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[大神神社]]の末社。加屋奈流美神、事代主神、八尋鰐を祀る。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm カヤナルミ考1] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html 飛鳥坐神社とは｜飛鳥坐神社公式ホームページ] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 加夜奈留美命神社(かやなるみのみことじんじや)とは？ 意味や使い方 - コトバンク] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html 大和国一宮・三輪明神 大神神社 奈良県] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かやなるみ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:大神氏]] [[Category:女神]]

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].

=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. === Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
