{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)

The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Asuka Shrine (Nara)|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

<!--Kayanarumi no Kami--> [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Goddesses]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:カヤナルミ]] [[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11294873]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

<!--Kayanarumi no Kami--> [[Category:Miwa clan]] [[Category:Goddesses]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:カヤナルミ]] [[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{wikidata link|Q11294873}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11294873]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11294873]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11294873]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11294873]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>

In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).

She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11294873]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:18, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka Village]], Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka Village}}, Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City}}, where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>

The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).

* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka Village}}, Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City}}, where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:カヤナルミ]][[zh:賀夜奈流美命]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233|1=Shitateruhime}}}}
{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Deity type = [[Kunitsukami|国津神]] | Full name = 賀夜奈流美命 (Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto) | Other names = 加夜奈留美命, 賀屋鳴比女 | Rank = | Attributes = | Burial place = | Father = | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Siblings = | Children = | Shrine = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (末社: 大行事社) }} | Texts = [[Engishiki]] | Related clans = [[Ōmiwa clan|大神氏]] }} '''Kayanarumi''' (賀夜奈流美, also written as 加夜奈留美 or 賀屋鳴比女) is a [[Kami|Deity]] (kami) mentioned in the ancient text ''[[Engishiki]]''. The deity appears in the ''[[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto]]'' (Izumo Province 造神賀詞), a ritual prayer recorded in the text. ''Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto'' is primarily enshrined as a goddess at {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Kayanarumi does not appear in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and little is known about her origins, meaning, or legends.
=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka Village|1=Asuka, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.dai3gen.net/kayanrm.htm Kayanarumi Studies] * [http://asukaniimasujinja.jp/about/index.html About Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine] * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8A%A0%E5%A4%9C%E5%A5%88%E7%95%99%E7%BE%8E%E5%91%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3081759 Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine - Kotobank] * [http://mobile.oomiwa.or.jp/daigyou.html Daigyōji-sha - Ōmiwa Shrine Official]

=== Etymology === One theory suggests the name "Narumi" (鳴海) might symbolize "resonating sea," linking the deity to the concepts of sound and water. Scholars have speculated connections to male deities such as [[Torinarumi-no-Kami]] (鳥鳴海神) and [[Nunoshitomi Torinarumi]] (布忍富鳥鳴海神), both mentioned in the genealogy of [[Ōkuninushi]], though the exact relationship remains unclear.<ref>Shinchō Nihon Koten Shūsei, Kojiki: Kami Name Interpretations</ref>
=== Appearance in Texts === In the ''Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko no Kamuyogoto,'' Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is described as one of the guardian deities protecting the [[:en:Imperial family|Imperial family]] alongside [[Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ajisukitakahikone|Ajisukitakahikone-no-Mikoto]], and [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto]]. The text states:
* "賀夜奈流美命乃御魂乎飛鳥乃神奈備尓坐天皇孫命能近守神登貢置天" (*The soul of Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto resides in the sacred mountain of Asuka, protecting the Imperial descendants.*)
In ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'' (January 27, 859 CE), Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto was elevated from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (従五位下) to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (正四位下).
== Shrines == Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto is enshrined in several locations in Nara Prefecture:
* '''{{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|qq=}}''': Located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka Village|1=Asuka, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture. She is worshiped as the main deity. * '''{{ill|Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Situated in Kashiwano, Asuka Village. * '''{{ill|Kyobate Tenjinsha|ja|飛鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyobate Tenjinsha|qq=}}''': Located in Nara City, enshrining Kayanarumi along with deities such as [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Ustukihime]], [[Fusokurihime]], and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. * '''Daigyōji-sha (大行事社)''': A subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|qid=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, where Kayanarumi is enshrined alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] and the eight-headed crocodile deity (八尋鰐).
